# orcasecurity_integration_jira_cloud_resource (Data Source)

Looks up a connected **Jira Cloud site** by its `name`.

Jira Cloud credentials are created through the OAuth flow in the Orca UI (Settings →
Integrations → Jira Cloud), not through the API — so there is no matching managed
resource, only this read-only data source. Use it to feed the site's `id` (and `url`)
into [`orcasecurity_integration_jira_cloud_template`](../resources/integration_jira_cloud_template.md)
without hard-coding UUIDs.

Under the hood this reads `GET /api/jira/resources` and matches the returned sites by
name client-side.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up a connected Jira Cloud site by name and use its id as resource_id on a Jira Cloud
# template. Jira Cloud credentials are created via the OAuth flow in the Orca UI.
data "orcasecurity_integration_jira_cloud_resource" "existing" {
  name = "my-tenant"
}

resource "orcasecurity_integration_jira_cloud_template" "demo" {
  template_name = "my-jira-cloud-template"
  resource_id   = data.orcasecurity_integration_jira_cloud_resource.existing.id
  resource_url  = data.orcasecurity_integration_jira_cloud_resource.existing.url
  project_id    = "10000"
  issue_type_id = "10001"

  mapping_json = jsonencode({
    summary = ["alert_name", "alert_id"]
  })
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `name` — (Required, String) Name of the connected Jira Cloud site (its subdomain /
  display name in Orca). The data source fails if zero or more than one site match.

## Attribute Reference

* `id` — (String) Jira Cloud resource identifier (cloud id). Feed this into the
  `resource_id` argument on `orcasecurity_integration_jira_cloud_template`.
* `url` — (String) Base URL of the Jira Cloud site (usable as `resource_url` on the
  template).
